name: Willow Glen Terminal – St. Gabriel (Former Entergy Power Station)
notes:
PREMIER SITE. Willow Glen Terminal, 2605 LA-75, St. Gabriel, LA 70776. Former Entergy Willow Glen Power Station (decommissioned 2016), acquired by Zydeco Equity Holdings/Willow Glen Ventures in 2020. Total property is 700 acres with 400+ developable acres available for sale or lease (subdividable down to 1 acre). 3,500 linear feet of deep-water Mississippi River frontage with shared dock. On-property Entergy substation (230 kV and 138 kV) — a major 15-mile 230 kV transmission line project is underway from this site. On-property Canadian National Railway access. ON A MAJOR PIPELINE CORRIDOR with direct pipeline access. 2.24 million barrel tank farm. Price upon request — contact CBRE broker Bryce French or Max J. Derbes Inc. This is arguably the best site on the corridor: direct river frontage + direct pipeline access + on-site Entergy substation + rail + deep-water dock. ADC 3K could acquire a 5-50 acre slice for a data center campus with river water cooling intake and direct gas pipeline tap.

notes:
160 acres at $3,000/acre directly on the West Atchafalaya Basin Protection Levee. PIPELINE ON SITE: listing confirms "a pipeline traversing east to west throughout the tract." name: Tract B Hwy 190 – Port Allen (20-Acre Commercial Parcel)
notes:
Tract B, 190 Hwy, Port Allen, LA 70767. 20.3 acres of commercial-zoned land at $595,000 ($29,310/acre). Located on US Hwy 190 in Port Allen, directly across the Mississippi River from Baton Rouge. West Baton Rouge Parish pipeline corridor — numerous gas transmission lines serving refineries and chemical plants run through this area. The site is approximately 1.5 miles from the Mississippi River levee (not direct riverfront like the 33-acre tract nearby, but within the target corridor). Strong paved road access via Hwy 190. Pipeline proximity is strong — West Baton Rouge's refinery row (Placid Refining, Alon USA) has dense pipeline infrastructure.
